name: Human Connectome Project — Young Adult (HCP-YA)
description: The WU-Minn HCP 1200 Subjects release provides high-quality 3T (and 7T subset) multimodal MRI from 1,206 healthy young adults aged 22–35, including structural, resting-state and task fMRI, and diffusion imaging, plus extensive behavioral and demographic measures. Task fMRI includes social cognition (animated-shapes theory-of-mind), emotion, gambling, language, and working-memory paradigms, making it a cornerstone resource for social and cognitive neuroscience.
citation: Van Essen DC, Smith SM, Barch DM, Behrens TEJ, Yacoub E, Ugurbil K, WU-Minn HCP Consortium (2013). The WU-Minn Human Connectome Project: an overview. NeuroImage, 80, 62–79.

publications.title: The WU-Minn Human Connectome Project: an overview
